title: Rust orderbook / exchange simulator
summary: A Rust limit order book and exchange simulation with actor-driven order flow, pruning, websocket snapshots, and a live UI.

sections.heading: Why this exists
sections.body:
I started this as a way to understand order matching, but it turned into a larger question about where behavior should live: the order book, the exchange layer, the actors, or the UI.

The point is not to pretend this is a production exchange. The point is to use the project as a pressure chamber for Rust, systems design, market structure, and tradeoffs.
